title: Empowering LLMs by hybrid retrieval-augmented generation for domain-centric Q&A in smart manufacturing

agents.description: A hybrid RAG agent that retrieves information from both a knowledge graph (Neo4j) and a vector store, applies semantic alignment with domain constraints, and generates domain-specific Q&A responses for smart manufacturing and DfAM tasks.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: The system follows a single-step orchestration pattern where a user query triggers a layered hybrid retrieval pipeline: (1) KG-based retrieval via Neo4j graph traversal, (2) vector-based retrieval via semantic similarity search if KG results are insufficient, (3) semantic alignment with domain constraints, and (4) prompt-engineered generation using the LLM to produce a final answer.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Domain experts are involved in curating the initial document corpus, validating the constructed knowledge graph (reviewing mapped triplets, nodes, and edges), and conducting a human-in-the-loop validation step to ensure truthfulness and relevance of the knowledge base. End users interact with the system via natural language queries.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Domain-centric question answering for smart manufacturing, specifically applied to Design for Additive Manufacturing (DfAM), supporting decision-making around material selection, process optimization, geometric design, and performance metrics.
retrieval.problem_description: LLMs face domain gaps, hallucinations, and outdated knowledge in smart manufacturing Q&A. Conventional vector-based RAG lacks precision for domain-specific relationships, while KG-based RAG struggles with scalability and handling unstructured data. Neither approach alone is sufficient for accurate, context-aware responses in complex manufacturing domains.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A hybrid KG-Vector RAG framework that integrates structured knowledge graph metadata (Neo4j) with unstructured vector embeddings (text-embedding-3-small). The system constructs a metadata-enriched KG from domain corpora, applies semantic alignment via domain-specific constraints and terminology injection, and employs a layered hybrid retrieval strategy combining KG graph traversal with vector similarity search, enhanced by prompt engineering, to generate comprehensive domain-specific responses.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: A controlled comparative experiment evaluating four RAG strategies (vector-based, graph-based, KG-Vector hybrid, KG-Vector with prompt engineering) across three LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, DeepSeek V2) on a DfAM dataset of 69 publications, using generic metrics (exact match, context precision, latency) and domain metrics (LLM-as-judge scoring).

decision_quality_improvement: GPT-4o with KG-Vector RAG + PE achieved 77.8% exact match accuracy, up from 63.4% with vector-only RAG.
GPT-4o with KG-Vector RAG + PE achieved 76.5% context precision, up from 69.8% with vector-only RAG.
GPT-4o mini with KG-Vector RAG + PE achieved 75.5% EM and 74.2% CP.
DeepSeek V2 with KG-Vector RAG + PE achieved 74.8% EM and 73.9% CP.
Domain metric (LLM-as-judge): GPT-4o with KG-Vector + PE achieved highest average score of 7.83/10.
Incremental improvement observed across all LLMs moving from vector-only to KG-only to hybrid to hybrid+PE.
response_time_impact: Vector-based RAG (Group 1) had lowest latency: DeepSeek V2 at 3.84s, GPT-4o mini at 6.07s, GPT-4o at 7.01s.
KG-based RAG (Group 2) increased latency: DeepSeek V2 at 4.86s, GPT-4o mini at 8.22s, GPT-4o at 9.46s.
Hybrid KG-Vector RAG (Group 3) further increased latency: DeepSeek V2 at 5.79s, GPT-4o mini at 9.99s, GPT-4o at 10.96s.
KG-Vector RAG with PE (Group 4) had highest latency: DeepSeek V2 at 8.67s, GPT-4o mini at 14.37s, GPT-4o at 16.33s.
Prompt engineering introduces additional pre-processing steps that increase latency substantially.
reliability_metrics: Three separate trial scores reported for domain metric evaluation to assess consistency.
GPT-4o with KG-Vector + PE scored (8.0, 7.5, 8.0) across three trials.

ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: Hybrid KG-Vector RAG outperformed both vector-only and KG-only RAG across all metrics and LLMs, demonstrating the value of combining structured and unstructured retrieval.
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Prompt engineering (few-shot examples, domain constraints, tailored instructions) further improved EM from 71.x% to 77.8% and CP from 71.x% to 76.5% for GPT-4o.
ce_technique_effectiveness.chain-of-thought: Layered retrieval and reasoning process (KG first, then vector if needed, then consolidation) improved recall and accuracy in complex multi-faceted DfAM queries.

failure_modes: Vector-based RAG fails to capture domain-specific relationships and specialized vocabulary, leading to contextually vague results.
KG-based RAG is limited by KG completeness; if KG lacks certain information, comprehensive responses cannot be generated.
KG sparsity requires fallback to vector retrieval for queries not fully covered by the KG.
Prompt engineering increases latency significantly, creating a trade-off between accuracy and response time.
limitations: The proposed method is tailored to the smart manufacturing domain; knowledge collection and preparation must be adjusted for other domains.
Keeping KGs up-to-date with emerging domain-specific terms and recent advancements remains a critical challenge.
Higher latency in hybrid and PE-enhanced approaches may limit suitability for real-time applications.
The system depends heavily on the quality and relevance of the underlying knowledge base.
Automated KG update strategies are not yet implemented; manual expert validation is required.

identified_research_gaps: Adapting the proposed method for broader domain applications beyond smart manufacturing by refining the process of knowledge collection and preparation
Improving the timeliness of knowledge graphs through automated KG update strategies to integrate emerging domain-specific terms and recent advancements
Dynamically updating external knowledge sources to improve the system's adaptability and reliability in fast-changing environments
